objective: Single-page boot-waterfall UI. Renders the boot-span chain from any node as a horizontal cascade. Vanilla HTML+CSS+JS, no SPA framework, no topology graph, no alerts panel, no heartbeat panel.
design_reference: docs/design/system-mesh-v1/ — long-term vision. Sprint 07 ships ONLY the boot waterfall slice. Topology graph + alerts + heartbeat + type cards are explicitly deferred (likely never built for v1).
in_scope: topology-ui/ binary — axum server on http://localhost:19090 (read RAFKA_TOPOLOGY_UI_BIND_ADDR env var, default 127.0.0.1:19090)
Single HTML page: header + waterfall canvas + node-selector dropdown
Polls Jaeger query API for traces matching service=rafkav2-* operation=rafka.mesh.node.ready
Renders trace as horizontal waterfall: each span is a colored div, width = duration, x-offset = start_time relative to root, vertical position by parent-child nesting
Color-codes by span phase (identity / endpoint / alpn / gossip / accept_loop) per the existing palette in docs/design/system-mesh-v1/project/styles.css
Node selector dropdown lets user pick which node's most-recent boot trace to view
out_of_scope: Topology graph (deferred indefinitely; mesh visualization is not v1 scope)
Active alerts panel (no alert entity exists)
OTLP heartbeat panel (no gateway-side span-rate aggregation)
Type-card drill-down (no fleet to drill into)
WebSocket live updates (polling is fine — boot is a one-shot event)
Any SPA framework, build pipeline, transpilation, node_modules
exit_criteria: cargo run -p rafka-topology-ui starts UI on http://localhost:19090
Open UI in browser, select node from dropdown
See the 6-span boot waterfall rendered as horizontal bars with timing labels
Boot a SECOND node → it appears in the dropdown within 10s (UI re-polls Jaeger periodically)
All UI HTTP requests instrumented with spans (service=rafkav2-topology-ui)
Jaeger URL pre-filtered to the UI's own traces
discipline: NO SPA framework — vanilla HTML + CSS + JS only (Principle #10 KISS)
NO topology graph in this sprint — that's the scope-creep trap; resist
Polling, not WebSocket — boot waterfall is a one-shot view, not a live stream
All config via env vars (Principle #8): RAFKA_TOPOLOGY_UI_BIND_ADDR + JAEGER_QUERY_URL (default http://localhost:16686)
Closes with Jaeger URL per Principle #7
